title: Should Early Ranking Targets Be Decomposed for Compositional Text-to-Image Generation?
description: This project is a controlled empirical study of early diffusion ranking targets for compositional text-to-image generation. It tests whether, under a fixed prune-continue budget and matched early features, ranking partial trajectories by benchmark-aligned atomic constraints works better than ranking them with a single scalar compositional score.
motivation: Recent work already shows that early diffusion signals can predict final image quality, and separate work shows that compositional generation often benefits from condition-level scoring. The remaining gap is narrower and scientifically cleaner: for compositional prompts, should early continuation targets be decomposed into atomic constraints, or is a scalar target sufficient once features, model size, and compute are controlled?
proposed_approach: Freeze Stable Diffusion v1.5, parse GenEval-style prompts into deterministic count, attribute-binding, and relation constraints, and run multiple seeds only through a short prefix such as 4 of 20 denoising steps. From shared early features built from attention statistics, denoiser disagreement, and one low-resolution preview embedding, train two matched probes: a decomposed probe with one head per atomic constraint type and a scalar probe with one overall success target. Train both primarily on benchmark-aligned completed-image labels, then use a small manually verified subset to estimate label noise and check calibration sensitivity. Under an exact B40 budget, continue only the top-ranked partial trajectory and compare decomposed-versus-scalar ranking on a fixed held-out GenEval split.

related_work.title: Toward Early Quality Assessment of Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
related_work.authors: Huanlei Guo, Hongxin Wei, Bingyi Jing
related_work.how_it_differs: This is the closest early-assessment scaffold, but it studies generic early quality prediction rather than whether compositional early ranking targets should be decomposed.

related_work.title: Diffusion Probe: Generated Image Result Prediction Using CNN Probes
related_work.authors: Benlei Cui, Bukun Huang, Zhizeng Ye, Xuemei Dong, Tuo Chen, Hui Xue, Dingkang Yang, Longtao Huang, Jingqun Tang, Haiwen Hong
related_work.how_it_differs: Also predicts final outcomes from early diffusion signals, but does not isolate decomposed versus scalar targets for compositional continuation.

related_work.title: Improving Compositional Generation with Diffusion Models Using Lift Scores
related_work.how_it_differs: Already argues for condition-level compositional scoring, which substantially narrows novelty; this proposal moves that question to the early prefix and tests it in a strictly matched prune-continue setting.

related_work.title: Inference-Time Scaling for Diffusion Models beyond Scaling Denoising Steps
related_work.authors: Nanye Ma, Shangyuan Tong, Haolin Jia, Hexiang Hu, Yu-Chuan Su, Mingda Zhang, Xuan Yang, Yandong Li, Tommi Jaakkola, Xuhui Jia, Saining Xie
related_work.how_it_differs: Uses more elaborate inference-time search and verification, whereas this proposal changes only the early ranking target under a small fixed budget.

related_work.title: Fine-Grained Alignment and Noise Refinement for Compositional Text-to-Image Generation
related_work.authors: Amir Mohammad Izadi, Seyed Mohammad Hadi Hosseini, Soroush Vafaie Tabar, Ali Abdollahi, Armin Saghafian, Mahdieh Soleymani Baghshah
related_work.how_it_differs: Applies verifier-guided correction and refinement after stronger generation feedback rather than testing target structure for early continuation.

related_work.title: Iterative Refinement Improves Compositional Image Generation
related_work.authors: Shantanu Jaiswal, Mihir Prabhudesai, Nikash Bhardwaj, Zheyang Qin, Amir Zadeh, Chuan Li, Katerina Fragkiadaki, Deepak Pathak
related_work.how_it_differs: Represents a heavier critic-guided refinement alternative; the present proposal is a lightweight target-comparison study on frozen generation.

related_work.title: Divide and Conquer: Language Models can Plan and Self-Correct for Compositional Text-to-Image Generation
related_work.authors: Zhenyu Wang, Enze Xie, Aoxue Li, Zhongdao Wang, Xihui Liu, Zhenguo Li
related_work.how_it_differs: Uses planning and self-correction to alter generation, while this proposal keeps the generator fixed and only changes which partial trajectory is continued.

related_work.title: Training-Free Structured Diffusion Guidance for Compositional Text-to-Image Synthesis
related_work.authors: Weixi Feng, Xuehai He, Tsu-Jui Fu, Varun Jampani, Arjun Akula, Pradyumna Narayana, Sugato Basu, Xin Eric Wang, William Yang Wang
related_work.how_it_differs: Modifies the sampling dynamics to improve compositionality, whereas this study focuses on early ranking targets with no sampler change.

related_work.title: GenEval: An Object-Focused Framework for Evaluating Text-to-Image Alignment
related_work.authors: Dhruba Ghosh, Hanna Hajishirzi, Ludwig Schmidt
related_work.how_it_differs: Provides the main benchmark and atomic constraint vocabulary for the benchmark-specialized empirical test.

related_work.title: T2I-CompBench++: An Enhanced and Comprehensive Benchmark for Compositional Text-to-image Generation
related_work.authors: Kaiyi Huang, Chengqi Duan, Kaiyue Sun, Enze Xie, Zhenguo Li, Xihui Liu
related_work.how_it_differs: Serves only as an optional transfer check if measured latency shows that extra evaluation fits inside the eight-hour budget.

hypothesis: For compositional prompts with explicit atomic constraints, early diffusion features retain information that is useful at the constraint level and partly lost when collapsed into a single scalar success target. Therefore, under matched prefix length, features, probe capacity, and B40 compute budget, a decomposed early ranking target should outperform a matched scalar early target on a fixed held-out GenEval split.
success_criteria: On the fixed 80-prompt held-out GenEval test set at B40, the decomposed early target outperforms the matched scalar early target built from the same early features, model capacity, and continuation rule.
The main decomposed-versus-scalar effect survives prompt-level uncertainty analysis, such as paired bootstrap confidence intervals or a paired permutation test.
The method is competitive with or better than a practical best-of-2 full-completion baseline selected by a generic preference model at the same B40 budget.
A small manually verified subset of completed train or validation images supports the calibration and label-noise assumptions rather than contradicting them.
Optional transfer or human-check results are only claimed if measured A6000 latencies show that those additions fit cleanly within the eight-hour runtime budget.
